name: Crisp API
what_it_does: Crisp's REST API for managing live chat conversations, contacts, campaigns, and automated messaging across websites and mobile apps.
use_cases: Building AI chatbot integrations that respond to customer chat messages automatically
Querying and updating conversation status and metadata from support automation agents
Syncing contact data between Crisp and CRM systems programmatically
Triggering outbound chat campaigns or proactive messages based on user behavior
Routing conversations to the right team based on agent-evaluated conversation content
not_for: Teams needing enterprise ticketing with SLA management (use Zendesk or Freshdesk)
Email-only support workflows where live chat is irrelevant
Large call centers requiring voice or omnichannel queue management
best_when: An agent needs to participate in or automate live chat conversations on a website, particularly for small-to-mid sized businesses seeking affordable Intercom alternatives.
avoid_when: You need advanced enterprise ITSM features, complex SLA management, or multi-channel support beyond chat and email.

auth.notes: Uses HTTP Basic Auth with Identifier (API key ID) as username and Key (secret) as password. Generated per operator or at website level. No OAuth or token scoping available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited seats, 2 agents active simultaneously, basic chat features
pricing.notes: Generous free tier by live chat standards. Pro adds unlimited active agents and automations. Unlimited adds CRM, campaigns, and analytics. Pricing per website, not per seat, which is unusual.

performance.rate_limits: 600 requests/hour per API key pair; RTM websocket for real-time events

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include reason and error fields but coverage is inconsistent. Some undocumented 4xx errors surface in production use. Error format is not standardized across all endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys or deduplication support. Duplicate API calls create duplicate messages. Agents must track state externally to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Real-time events require RTM (Real-Time Messaging) WebSocket connection, not REST polling — REST alone will miss live messages
API key pair (identifier + key) is two separate values, not one; many agents fail to split them correctly for Basic Auth
Rate limit of 600 requests/hour is low for active chat automation — throttle carefully or use webhooks instead
Message sending requires session_id which must be fetched first; no shortcut to send without a prior lookup
Webhook event types are not fully documented; some events only discoverable by inspection of live traffic
Website-scoped vs operator-scoped API keys behave differently in terms of conversation visibility

security_score_components.security_notes: Data hosted in EU, which satisfies GDPR requirements. API keys have no fine-grained scopes. Key pairs should be stored securely; if compromised, they provide full account access.
